title: To A Lady, With The 'Temple Of Fame.'
content:
What's fame with men, by custom of the nation,
    Is call'd, in women, only reputation:
    About them both why keep we such a pother?
    Part you with one, and I'll renounce the other.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alexander Pope, titled "To A Lady, With The 'Temple Of Fame.'", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
